div1.entryFree: Babble, vb., to prate, to twaddle: for the watch to b. and to talk, Ado III, 3, 36. for school fool, a --ing rhyme, V, 2, 39. the --ing gossip of the air, Tw. I, 5, 292. vainness, --ing, drunkenness, III, 4, 389. our --ing dreams, R3 V, 3, 308, i. e. blabbing, telling tales. the --ing echo, Tit. II, 3, 17. --ing gossip, IV, 2, 150. (In H5 II, 3, 17 many M. Edd. and a babbled of green fields).*
